description:
You are a technical writer who keeps all documentation under “/home/user/docs”.  
At the moment that directory contains three draft-version text files:

1. /home/user/docs/faq_draft.txt  
2. /home/user/docs/intro_draft.txt  
3. /home/user/docs/usage_draft.txt  

Each of those files must be renamed so their suffix “_draft.txt” becomes “_v1.txt”, e.g. “faq_draft.txt” → “faq_v1.txt”.  
Perform the entire batch rename with **one single shell command that combines “find” and “xargs”** (you may embed other standard GNU utilities inside that single command line, but it must be one command).

Logging requirement  
For every file that is renamed, append a line to “/home/user/docs/rename.log” whose exact format is:

old_filename -> new_filename

Use only the base filenames (no directory path).  
The lines in /home/user/docs/rename.log must be written in strict lexicographic (ASCII) order of the original filenames and must be terminated by exactly one newline each, with no extra blank lines at the end of the file.  
The expected final contents of /home/user/docs/rename.log are therefore:

faq_draft.txt -> faq_v1.txt  
intro_draft.txt -> intro_v1.txt  
usage_draft.txt -> usage_v1.txt  

After your command finishes:
• none of the “*_draft.txt” files should exist;  
• all three “*_v1.txt” files should exist and keep the exact text that had been in their draft counterparts;  
• the log file must exist with the precise content described above.

Remember: accomplish everything with a single terminal command that employs find and xargs. No manual moves, no additional sequential commands.
truth:
Initial filesystem state before the task starts
-----------------------------------------------
Directory: /home/user/docs

Files and their contents (each file ends with a single newline):

/home/user/docs/faq_draft.txt
--------------------------------
Frequently-asked questions draft.
--------------------------------

/home/user/docs/intro_draft.txt
--------------------------------
Introduction section draft.
--------------------------------

/home/user/docs/usage_draft.txt
--------------------------------
Usage instructions draft.
--------------------------------

There is no file named “rename.log” yet.

Expected filesystem state after correct task completion
-------------------------------------------------------

1. The three draft files no longer exist:
   /home/user/docs/faq_draft.txt  (should be absent)
   /home/user/docs/intro_draft.txt (should be absent)
   /home/user/docs/usage_draft.txt (should be absent)

2. Three new version-1 files exist with exactly the same textual contents as their respective draft versions:

/home/user/docs/faq_v1.txt
--------------------------------
Frequently-asked questions draft.
--------------------------------

/home/user/docs/intro_v1.txt
--------------------------------
Introduction section draft.
--------------------------------

/home/user/docs/usage_v1.txt
--------------------------------
Usage instructions draft.
--------------------------------

3. Log file:

/home/user/docs/rename.log
--------------------------------
faq_draft.txt -> faq_v1.txt
intro_draft.txt -> intro_v1.txt
usage_draft.txt -> usage_v1.txt
--------------------------------
(The file ends with a single trailing newline right after “usage_v1.txt”.)

Automated tests will verify:
• absence of *_draft.txt files;  
• presence of *_v1.txt files with byte-identical content to originals;  
• rename.log exists with exactly the three lines shown, in that order, and nothing else.
